title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.imageAlt: Platter of authentic Lamb Ouzi, whole slow roasted lamb served with spiced rice and nuts, garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, aromatic flavors of traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to perfection.

description: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a magnificent centerpiece of Middle Eastern cuisine, featuring tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb infused with aromatic spices. This celebratory dish combines succulent slow-roasted meat with fragrant, spiced rice, making it perfect for special gatherings and feasts. The rich flavors and impressive presentation promise an unforgettable dining experience.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, Seven Spice blend, ground cumin, ground coriander, sweet paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to form a paste.

instructions.title: Season and Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb shoulder dry. Make deep incisions all over the lamb with a sharp knife. Rub the spice paste generously into the lamb, ensuring it gets into the incisions. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan. Add bay leaves, cinnamon sticks, and quartered onions around the lamb. Cover tightly with foil. Roast in a preheated oven at 160°C (325°F) for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until very tender and falling off the bone. Remove foil for the last 30 minutes to brown the lamb.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spiced Rice Base
instructions.text: While the lamb roasts, heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add vermicelli noodles and cook, stirring constantly, until golden brown. Add the rinsed basmati rice and stir for 2-3 minutes until lightly toasted.

instructions.title: Cook the Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: Pour the chicken or lamb broth into the pot with the rice and vermicelli. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Once cooked, gently fold in the frozen green peas and cooked diced carrots.

instructions.title: Assemble the Lamb Ouzi
instructions.text: Once the lamb is cooked and rested, carefully transfer it to a large serving platter. Fluff the spiced rice with a fork and arrange it around the lamb, or on a separate large platter if serving family style.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish the Lamb Ouzi generously with toasted slivered almonds, fresh chopped parsley, and toasted pine nuts. Serve immediately as a magnificent centerpiece for your feast.

tips: For extra flavor, marinate the lamb overnight in the refrigerator. This allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat and enhances tenderness.
Use good quality basmati rice and rinse it thoroughly until the water runs clear. This prevents stickiness and ensures fluffy, separate grains for your ouzi.
The roasted lamb drippings can be skimmed of excess fat and served as a rich, savory gravy alongside the ouzi for an added layer of flavor.
article:
**Savoring Tradition: The Grandeur of Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi**

Few dishes evoke the warmth, generosity, and rich culinary heritage of the Middle East quite like Lamb Ouzi. This magnificent feast, often reserved for grand celebrations, weddings, and family gatherings, is more than just a meal; it's an experience. Imagine a whole lamb, slow-roasted to succulent perfection, its tender meat falling off the bone, served atop a fragrant bed of spiced rice, studded with nuts and often vegetables. It’s a dish that commands attention, tantalizes the senses, and promises an unforgettable journey into the heart of Middle Eastern hospitality. While the idea of roasting a whole lamb might seem daunting, this guide will demystify the process, inviting you to embrace the joy of creating this spectacular centerpiece for your next special occasion.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, also known as Ghozi or Khouzi in various regions, is a quintessential Middle Eastern rice dish featuring slow-cooked lamb or mutton. While variations exist, the most impressive and traditional presentation involves a whole lamb, or at least a significant portion like a leg or shoulder, roasted until incredibly tender. The meat is typically marinated in a blend of aromatic spices, then slow-cooked, often for many hours, allowing its flavors to deepen and its texture to become exquisitely soft. The star accompaniment is a highly seasoned rice pilaf, often enriched with ground lamb or beef, toasted nuts (pine nuts, almonds, pistachios), and sometimes dried fruits like raisins. This vibrant rice is then either stuffed into the cavity of the whole roasted lamb or, more commonly, served as a lavish bed upon which the roasted meat is presented. The sheer scale and intricate flavors of Lamb Ouzi make it a symbol of abundance and celebration, a true culinary marvel that brings people together around a shared table.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Crafting an authentic Lamb Ouzi requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients, ensuring both flavor and tenderness.

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb (approx. 20-30 lbs), or a large lamb leg/shoulder (5-7 lbs)
*   Olive oil
*   Salt and freshly ground black pepper
*   Spice rub: 2 tbsp ground cumin, 2 tbsp ground coriander, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp turmeric, 1 tsp ground cardamom, 1 tsp allspice, 1 tsp dried thyme, 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper (optional)
*   Aromatics: 1 large onion, quartered; 6-8 cloves garlic, smashed; fresh rosemary or bay leaves

For the Ouzi Rice (Pilaf):
*   4 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   1 lb ground lamb or beef (optional, for richer flavor)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   4 tbsp olive oil or ghee
*   4-6 cups lamb broth or water
*   Spice blend for rice: 1 tbsp allspice, 1 tsp ground cinnamon, 1 tsp ground cardamom, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1/2 tsp ground cloves
*   1 cup mixed nuts, toasted (pine nuts, slivered almonds, pistachios)
*   1/2 cup dried cranberries or raisins (optional)
*   Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish
*   Salt to taste

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Lamb Ouzi

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. Score the skin deeply in a diamond pattern, being careful not to cut into the meat too much. In a small bowl, combine all the spice rub ingredients. Rub the lamb generously with olive oil, then sprinkle and massage the spice rub all over, ensuring it gets into the scores. Stuff the cavity with quartered onion, smashed garlic, and fresh herbs. Truss the lamb if necessary, to help it cook evenly.
2.  **Marinate (Optional but Recommended):** For best results, cover the lamb and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, allowing the flavors to penetrate.
3.  **Preheat Oven & Roast:** Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C). Place the lamb on a rack in a large roasting pan. Add 2-3 cups of water or broth to the bottom of the pan to keep the lamb moist and create delicious pan drippings. Cover the entire pan tightly with foil.
4.  **Slow Roasting:** Roast for 4-6 hours for a whole lamb, or 3-4 hours for a large leg/shoulder, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare, or 160°F (71°C) for well-done (remember it will continue to cook while resting). Remove the foil for the last 30-60 minutes to allow the skin to crisp up. The meat should be incredibly tender and easily pull away from the bone.
5.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a carving board, and tent it loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 30 minutes (longer for a whole lamb) before carving. This is crucial for juicy, tender meat.
6.  **Prepare the Ouzi Rice:** While the lamb rests, prepare the rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil or ghee over medium heat. If using, brown the ground lamb/beef until cooked through, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the rice spice blend and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
7.  **Cook the Rice:** Add the rinsed basmati rice to the pot and stir to coat. Pour in the lamb broth or water, add salt to taste, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Do not lift the lid during cooking.
8.  **Finish the Rice:** Remove the pot from heat and let it sit, covered, for another 10 minutes. Fluff the rice with a fork, then gently fold in the toasted nuts and dried fruits (if using).
9.  **Assemble and Serve:** Carve the slow-roasted lamb, or simply pull large chunks of meat from the bone. Spread the fragrant Ouzi rice on a large serving platter. Arrange the lamb pieces artfully on top of the rice. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley and a sprinkle of extra toasted nuts.

## Expert Tips for Perfect Lamb Ouzi

*   **Quality Lamb is Key:** Invest in good quality lamb. Free-range or grass-fed lamb will yield superior flavor and texture.
*   **Low and Slow is the Way:** The secret to tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb is low heat and a long cooking time. Don't rush the roasting process. This allows the connective tissues to break down slowly, resulting in unparalleled tenderness.
*   **Don't Skip the Rest:** Resting the lamb after roasting allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring every bite is moist and flavorful. Cutting into it too soon will result in dry meat.
*   **Layer Flavors in Rice:** Sautéing the spices with the onions before adding the rice helps to bloom their flavors, creating a more aromatic and complex pilaf.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Toasting the nuts before adding them to the rice enhances their flavor and adds a delightful crunch. You can do this in a dry pan on the stovetop or in a moderate oven.
*   **Use Pan Drippings:** The liquid collected at the bottom of the roasting pan is liquid gold. Skim off excess fat and use the flavorful drippings to moisten the carved lamb or even drizzle over the rice.
*   **Consider Brining:** For an even more tender and flavorful lamb, especially for larger cuts, consider a simple salt brine a day before roasting.

## Serving Suggestions for Ouzi

The beauty of Lamb Ouzi lies in its completeness, often served as the undisputed star of the meal. However, a few accompaniments can elevate the experience:

*   **Fresh Salads:** A crisp, refreshing salad like Fattoush or Tabbouleh provides a vibrant contrast to the rich meat and rice. Their tangy dressings and fresh herbs cut through the richness beautifully.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like a deconstructed Tzatziki) or plain labneh offers a cool, creamy counterpoint that complements the spiced lamb perfectly.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) add a delightful sour crunch, balancing the savory elements.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice over the lamb brightens the flavors and adds a zesty finish.
*   **Extra Nuts and Herbs:** Always have extra toasted nuts and fresh parsley or cilantro on hand for a final flourish before serving. This adds visual appeal and a burst of freshness.

## Variations & Substitutions

While the whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi is a showstopper, there are many ways to adapt this recipe to suit different needs and preferences:

*   **Different Cuts of Lamb:** If a whole lamb is too ambitious, a large bone-in leg of lamb or lamb shoulder works wonderfully. The cooking time will be shorter, but the principles of slow roasting remain the same. You can also use lamb shanks for individual portions.
*   **Ground Meat Ouzi:** For a weeknight-friendly version, you can simply make the Ouzi rice with ground lamb or beef and serve it with grilled or pan-fried lamb chops, or even shredded leftover lamb.
*   **Vegetarian Rice:** Omit the ground lamb/beef from the rice and focus on the aromatic spices, nuts, and perhaps add some roasted vegetables like carrots or peas for a hearty vegetarian pilaf.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Feel free to adjust the spice levels to your liking. Add more cayenne for heat, or extra cardamom for a more aromatic profile.
*   **Nut Alternatives:** If you have nut allergies, simply omit them. Sunflower or pumpkin seeds can be a good substitute for crunch.
*   **Dried Fruits:** While optional, dried cranberries, apricots, or golden raisins add a lovely sweetness and chewiness to the rice, balancing the savory spices.
*   **Pressure Cooker/Instant Pot:** For faster cooking of the lamb, you can use a pressure cooker or Instant Pot for the initial tenderizing stage, then finish it in the oven for browning and crisping.

Lamb Ouzi is more than just a recipe; it's a culinary journey that culminates in a dish of unparalleled flavor and communal joy. Whether you embark on the grand feat of roasting a whole lamb or opt for a simpler cut, the essence of this Middle Eastern masterpiece lies in its rich spices, tender meat, and the spirit of sharing. Prepare to impress your guests and create lasting memories with this truly spectacular meal.
